title: senml: skip_one_depth arithmetic overflow on 16-bit platforms
description:
file: rust/lichen-senml/src/cbor.rs:382

The calculation `pos + adv + val as usize` in skip_one_depth can overflow on 16-bit platforms when val is large (up to 65535 from 2-byte length encoding). This is the same class of bug as k1wt (dec_text truncation) but in skip_one_depth.

On 16-bit platforms where usize is 16 bits:
- If val=65535, adv=3, pos=10, then 10+3+65535=65548 wraps to 12
- The bounds check `end > data.len()` would pass incorrectly
- Returns wrong advancement count, corrupting subsequent parsing

Impact: Incorrect CBOR parsing on 16-bit embedded targets when processing byte/text strings with 2-byte lengths.
close_reason: Fixed: Added checked arithmetic to skip_one_depth for major types 4 (arrays) and 5 (maps). Lines 393-408 now use pos.checked_add(adv) and cur.checked_add(skip) to prevent overflow on 16-bit platforms.
